dmTitle.techName: Moisture and Air Contaminant Control System

displayText.simplePara: AMPEL360e Wide Tube-and-Wing Family (eWTW) — All variants

remarks.simplePara: Programme-controlled placeholder. infoCode 040A subject to BREX/SNS/DMRL freeze. Not for operational use.

levelledPara.title: General
levelledPara.para: This data module describes the Moisture and Air Contaminant Control System of the AMPEL360e eWTW ECS, aligned to ATLAS node 020-070 and ATA 21-70. This subsystem manages cabin air humidity, prevents condensation accumulation in structure and equipment areas, and removes biological and chemical contaminants from recirculated cabin air.

levelledPara.title: Water Separation and Humidity Control
levelledPara.para: Water separators are installed in the ECS pack outlets to remove liquid water from the conditioned air supply before it enters the distribution system. The ECS does not actively humidify the cabin air in the baseline architecture; cabin humidity is a result of pack outlet conditions and passenger moisture generation.

listItem.para: Pack water separators — centrifugal separation of condensed water from cool pack outlet air.
Water ejectors — discharge separated water overboard or to the drain system.
Condensation drain paths — prevent pooling in structure and equipment bays.
Optional cabin humidifier (premium variant) — controlled water injection into supply air.

levelledPara.title: Air Filtration and Contaminant Control
levelledPara.para: Recirculated cabin air passes through HEPA (High Efficiency Particulate Air) filters before re-entering the distribution system. The HEPA filtration system removes particulates, micro-organisms and certain airborne contaminants. Filter maintenance intervals are defined in the Aircraft Maintenance Manual.
The air contaminant control system also includes:

listItem.para: Gaseous contaminant monitoring sensors (optional advanced air quality package).
Ozone converter on applicable variants (high-altitude cruise ozone reduction).
Smoke and fume detection interfaces (see fire protection system for crew alerting).

levelledPara.title: eWTW Air Quality Monitoring Integration
levelledPara.para: The eWTW aircraft integrates air quality sensors with the ECS monitoring and diagnostics system (see DMC-AMPEL360E-EWTW-020-080-00A-040A-D). Sensor data can be logged and trended via the aircraft health monitoring system to support predictive maintenance of filtration elements.
